_title: Signs your old self is dying
_raw_content: Before transformation completes, there is a collapse. The nervous system releases what it built its entire identity around. You don't feel enlightened. You feel like you're losing your mind. That disorientation is not a breakdown. It is the space between who you were and who you are becoming. The body shakes off decades of armor before it can stand naked in truth.
slides: You suddenly can't tolerate the life you built
Old friends feel like strangers wearing familiar skin
Your body is exhausted but nothing happened
You cry and don't know why and it feels ancient
The mask you wore for years won't fit your face anymore
You'd rather be alone than perform one more second
You feel like you're falling apart but something deeper knows you're falling into place
caption:
This is not a breakdown.

This is your nervous system releasing an identity it no longer needs.

The old self doesn't leave quietly. It thrashes. It grips. It makes you believe you're losing everything.

But what's actually happening is the body is finally safe enough to let go of what it carried for survival.

The disorientation is not the problem.

It is the passage.

You are not falling apart. You are falling out of a version of yourself that was never really you.

Let it die. What's underneath has been waiting your entire life.
image_prompt: Retro 90s anime style, close-up of a character with windswept hair staring into darkness, tears streaming but expression calm and powerful, dramatic backlighting in deep amber and violet, cinematic composition, grain texture, vibrant saturated colors, NO text NO words in image
